steps.title: Set up and expand the equation
steps.content:
Translate the phrase the product of a positive number $x$ and the number that is 15 less than $x$ into an equation. This gives us:

$$x(x - 15) = 286$$

Expand the left side:

$$x^2 - 15x = 286$$

Rearrange to standard form:

$$x^2 - 15x - 286 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor and apply the positivity constraint
steps.content:
To factor $x^2 - 15x - 286 = 0$, look for two numbers that multiply to $-286$ and add to $-15$. Notice that $286 = 26 \times 11$, and $26 - 11 = 15$. This means:

$$(x - 26)(x + 11) = 0$$

So $x = 26$ or $x = -11$. Since the problem states that $x$ is positive, the value of $x$ is \$26$.
